content.problemStatement:
Chef has found an integer array AAA of length NNN. He defines the beauty of an array as the maximal value of gcd⁡(Al,Al+1,…,Ar)⋅(r−l+1)\gcd(A_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_r) \cdot (r - l + 1)gcd(Al​,Al+1​,…,Ar​)⋅(r−l+1) over all the subsegments [l,r][l, r][l,r] of the array AAA.

Chef loves to explore, so aside from the beauty of the initial array, he wants to know the beauty of the array after performing each of QQQ updates. Each update replaces a subsegment of the array with an arithmetic progression. Updates are cumulative.

Formally, in each update you are given four integers lll, rrr, xxx, and yyy. Then for each i∈[l,r]i\in [l, r]i∈[l,r], you must replace AiA_iAi​ with x+y(i−l)x+y(i-l)x+y(i−l).


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and QQQ - the length of the array and the number of updates.
The second line contains NNN integers - the array AAA.
The next QQQ lines each contains four integers lll, rrr, xxx, yyy - the description of an update: an arithmetic progression with the initial term xxx and the common difference yyy on the subsegment [l,r][l, r][l,r].


Output Format
For each test case, output Q+1Q+1Q+1 lines. In the first line for each test case output the beauty of the initial array, in the iii-th (2≤i≤Q+12 \le i \le Q + 12≤i≤Q+1) line for each test case output the beauty of the array after performing i−1i-1i−1 changes.
constraints: It is guaranteed that after each update, no element of AAA is greater than 10910^9109.
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105.
The sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
After the first operation A=[2,6,4,12,18,12]A = [2, 6, 4, 12, 18, 12]A=[2,6,4,12,18,12]. The maximum value is reached on the segment [4,6][4, 6][4,6].
After the second operation A=[2,2,6,10,14,12]A = [2, 2, 6, 10, 14, 12]A=[2,2,6,10,14,12]. The maximum value is reached on the segment [5,5][5, 5][5,5].
